ucs.title: [LLM] CopyKittens (G0052) Matryoshka-style rundll32 DLL load from user-writable path chained to hidden PowerShell
ucs.description: Hunts CopyKittens' canonical Operation Wilted Tulip execution chain — Matryoshka/TDTESS DLL payloads launched via rundll32.exe from %AppData%, %ProgramData%, \Users\Public, or %Temp%, followed within 5 minutes by powershell.exe with -WindowStyle Hidden / -EncodedCommand on the same host & user. The combination (T1218.011 + T1059.001 + T1564.003) is documented for this Iranian cluster by ClearSky and Trend Micro.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as RundllTime values(Processes.process) as RundllCmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as RundllParent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" (Processes.process="*\\appdata\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\programdata\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\users\\public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\windows\\temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\local\\temp\\*") NOT Processes.user="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user | rename Processes.* as * | join type=inner dest user [ | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as PsTime values(Processes.process) as PsCmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as PsParent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe") (Processes.process="*-WindowStyle Hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-w hidden*" OR Processes.process="*-WindowStyl*" OR Processes.process="*-EncodedCommand*" OR Processes.process="*-enc *" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*IEX*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-Expression*") by Processes.dest Processes.user | rename Processes.* as * ] | where PsTime>=RundllTime AND PsTime<=(RundllTime+300) | table RundllTime PsTime dest user RundllParent RundllCmd PsParent PsCmd | sort - RundllTime
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let UserWritable = dynamic([@"\appdata\", @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\windows\temp\", @"\local\temp\"]);
let RundllStaged = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where tolower(ProcessCommandLine) has_any (UserWritable)
       or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)rundll32(\.exe)?\s+[^,\s\"]+\.dll[, ]+(#?\d+|[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,15})\b"
    | project RundllTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              RundllParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              RundllCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
              RundllPid    = ProcessId;
let HiddenPS = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
       or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand", "-enc ", "-ec ", "DownloadString", "IEX", "Invoke-Expression")
    | project PsTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, AccountName,
              PsParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              PsCmd    = ProcessCommandLine;
RundllStaged
| join kind=inner HiddenPS on DeviceId, AccountName
| where PsTime between (RundllTime .. RundllTime + 5m)
| project RundllTime, PsTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', PsTime, RundllTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          RundllParent, RundllCmd, PsParent, PsCmd
| order by RundllTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let UserWritable = dynamic([@"\appdata\", @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\windows\temp\", @"\local\temp\"]);
let RundllStaged = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\rundll32.exe"
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where tolower(CommandLine) has_any (UserWritable)
       or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)rundll32(\.exe)?\s+[^,\s\"]+\.dll[, ]+(#?\d+|[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,15})\b"
    | project RundllTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              RundllParent = ParentProcessName,
              RundllCmd    = CommandLine;
let HiddenPS = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName has_any (@"\powershell.exe", @"\pwsh.exe")
    | where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?"
       or CommandLine has_any ("-EncodedCommand", "-enc ", "-ec ", "DownloadString", "IEX", "Invoke-Expression")
    | project PsTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              PsParent = ParentProcessName,
              PsCmd    = CommandLine;
RundllStaged
| join kind=inner HiddenPS on Computer, Account
| where PsTime between (RundllTime .. RundllTime + 5m)
| project RundllTime, PsTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', PsTime, RundllTime),
          Computer, Account,
          RundllParent, RundllCmd, PsParent, PsCmd
| order by RundllTime desc
ucs.rationale: ClearSky's CopyKittens reports document Matryoshka and TDTESS dropping a DLL into %AppData% / %ProgramData% and re-launching it via rundll32 with an export ordinal, then handing off to PowerShell with -WindowStyle Hidden for the second-stage download. Generic 'PowerShell hidden' or 'rundll32 from temp' fire on commodity malware too — joining the two within a 5-minute same-user window is the signat

ucs.title: [LLM] CopyKittens pre-exfil staging: scripted RAR/7-Zip multi-volume password-protected archive of collected data
ucs.description: Hunts the CopyKittens collection→exfil pivot from Operation Wilted Tulip — rar.exe / WinRAR / 7z spawned non-interactively by powershell, cmd, wscript, cscript, mshta, or rundll32 (i.e. driven by the implant, not a user clicking RAR), with the password (-hp/-p) and split-volume (-v) flags the group uses to chunk staged data before egress. Tied directly to T1560.001 / T1560.003 in CopyKittens' MITRE technique list.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as image from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="rar.exe" OR Processes.process_name="winrar.exe" OR Processes.process_name="7z.exe" OR Processes.process_name="7za.exe" OR Processes.process_name="7zg.exe") (Processes.parent_process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="pwsh.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="cmd.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="wscript.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="cscript.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="mshta.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="rundll32.exe" OR Processes.parent_process_name="wmiprvse.exe") (Processes.process="* a *" OR Processes.process="*-hp*" OR Processes.process="*-p*" OR Processes.process="*-v*" OR Processes.process="*-m5*" OR Processes.process="*-ssw*" OR Processes.process="*-r *") NOT Processes.user="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.* as * | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let Archivers = dynamic(["rar.exe", "winrar.exe", "7z.exe", "7za.exe", "7zg.exe"]);
let ScriptedParents = dynamic(["powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "cmd.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "wmiprvse.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName in~ (Archivers)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ScriptedParents)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" a ", "-hp", "-p", "-v", "-m5", "-ssw", "-r ")
| extend HasPasswordFlag = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(hp|p)[\"\S]"
| extend HasSplitFlag    = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-v\d+[kmg]?\b"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Archiver = FileName,
          ArchiverCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentName  = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          HasPasswordFlag, HasSplitFlag
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let Archivers = dynamic(["rar.exe", "winrar.exe", "7z.exe", "7za.exe", "7zg.exe"]);
let ScriptedParents = dynamic(["powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "cmd.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "wmiprvse.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ImageLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    @"\")[-1]))
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ImageLeaf  in (Archivers)
| where ParentLeaf in (ScriptedParents)
| where CommandLine has_any (" a ", "-hp", "-p", "-v", "-m5", "-ssw", "-r ")
| extend HasPasswordFlag = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-(hp|p)[\"\S]"
| extend HasSplitFlag    = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s-v\d+[kmg]?\b"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          Archiver = ImageLeaf,
          ArchiverCmd = CommandLine,
          ParentName  = ParentLeaf,
          ParentImage = ParentProcessName,
          HasPasswordFlag, HasSplitFlag
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: CopyKittens-style scripted RAR/7z password-protected archive staging
id: 9b4e7f4a-4cb1-4a0c-9c0d-c0py1k1ttens02
status: experimental
description: Detects archive utilities (rar/winrar/7z) spawned non-interactively by script-host or LOLBin parents with password and/or split-volume flags, matching CopyKittens (G0052) pre-exfil staging tradecraft from Operation Wilted Tulip.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0052/
  - https://www.clearskysec.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/07/Operation_Wilted_Tulip.pdf
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.collection
  - attack.exfiltration
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.t1560.003
  - attack.g0052
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rar.exe'
      - '\WinRAR.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\7za.exe'
      - '\7zg.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wmiprvse.exe'
  selection_flags:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -hp'
      - ' -p'
      - ' -v'
      - ' -m5'
      - ' -ssw'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and selection_parent and selection_flags and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Backup or DLP agents that legitimately call rar/7z from a service wrapper (whitelist by parent path / signing cert)
  - IT-driven scripted archive jobs (whitelist scheduled-task service accounts)
level: high
ucs.rationale: ClearSky's Wilted Tulip report explicitly calls out CopyKittens using RAR with password and split-volume flags, driven by their PowerShell/cmd-based implants (Matryoshka, Vminst), to chunk and password-protect staged data before exfiltration to CDN-fronted infrastructure (T1090). Hunting RAR/7z alone is too noisy; constraining the parent to script-hosts / LOLBins eliminates the IT-admin and user-d

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `HasSplitFlag` is not a canonical column of SecurityEvent.
